Scenario.title: Operation MidnightEclipse: Disruption of Communication Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Communications Simple A skilled threat actor group leveraged a publicly known vulnerability in a communications provider's internet-facing application to infiltrate their network. After establishing access, they executed malicious payloads to create persistence and ultimately disrupted core communication systems through data manipulation attacks.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Internet-facing assets", "Configuration management systems", "Web server services such as emails and databases"]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["Vulnerability exploit", "Application compromise", "Data and Command Integrity Attack"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-17144 [CWE-200]: Improper Input Validation in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS
stage.description: The attackers exploited a vulnerability in a public-facing application (CVE-2020-17144) by targeting non-production interfaces that were mistakenly left accessible, allowing them to gain initial access through a vulnerability exploit vector.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.kev: CVE-2019-6340 [CWE-200]: Improper Input Validation in Cisco Firepower Management Center
stage.description: Executed malicious payloads via command injection through compromised web application interfaces, deploying lightweight backdoors to maintain persistent access within the network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Altered critical configuration files and injected malicious routing rules through input data manipulation, causing service outages and traffic redirection to disrupt communication infrastructure.

Scenario.summary: Operation MidnightEclipse targeted a communications provider by exploiting a known vulnerability in their public-facing application, escalating privileges through script-based API exploitation, and ultimately disrupting operations via configuration tampering. The attack highlights the risks of unpatched systems and insufficient input validation controls in critical infrastructure environments.
